topic: HOL-HIERARCHY c2 — Part 2 internal restructure + new SC subsections 2.13/2.14/2.15 + V13 promotion + Part 1 tier framing
change_summary: Pre-integration adversarial review of EXP-450 holistic proposal package (6 patches P0-P6, status=ready-for-integration). Class=holistic; singleton not batched.
our_argument_summary: EXP-450 proposes six integration patches to address the c2 HOLISTIC-HOL-HIERARCHY findings (7 holes — 4 major, 2 moderate, 1 minor): P0 renumber post-Summary loose args 2.10/2.11/2.12 + relocate Summary to true end-of-Part-2; P1-P3 add new SC subsections 2.13 κ-divide-by-zero, 2.14 ring-magnet 1,660:1, 2.15 FSF version-unstable (surfacing WIN-012/013/014, WIN-053, WIN-044 into Part 2 structural arc); P4 promote V13 from 2.9 to 2.1 (flagged operator-choice-required with a lighter italic-emphasis alternative); P5 update Part 1 'How This Review Is Organized' Tier-1 paragraph to include 1.5/1.7/1.8/1.9 alongside Parts 2 and 2b; P6 add 'case is overdetermined' acknowledgement to Part 1.1 Overview per the advocate-mode preemptive recommendation. The proposal's quantitative claims (Part 2 length 58,717; SC count 20; subsection offsets; cited WIN findings) all verified against current data. The proposal is structurally sound and addresses real hierarchy bugs — but three of the six patches contain substantive issues that should be revised BEFORE integration.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: EXP-450 is a pre-integration prose proposal package — no dome-code automation involved. Verification path: cross-check EXP claims against current sections.json/wins.json/predictions.json content.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: P0 TLDR update conditional on whether P1-P3 land (12→15 count). P6 uses {{SELF_CONTRADICTED}} templater token (globally wired in generate-html.js:82 — EXP's caveat about adding to templater mapping is unnecessary, token already registered).
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Operator-choice-required flagging on P4. Three deferred items (HIER-PHASE2-05/06/07) appropriately routed to follow-on work or operator decision.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None substantive. Minor: P6's templater-mapping caveat is unnecessary (token already global).

discrepancies: Section 1.6 (Dielectric Foundation): the parent c2 review (HOLISTIC-HOL-HIERARCHY.c2.json hole 0) listed 1.5/1.6/1.7/1.8/1.9 (five sections) as 'Tier-1-class structural attacks.' EXP P5 silently narrows to 1.5/1.7/1.8/1.9 (four sections, drops 1.6). The narrowing IS defensible — 1.6 reads as a Tier-2-style 'real observations + bad interpretation' critique, not a structural attack using dome parameters — but the EXP does NOT document the asymmetry. See HOLE-05.

Cross-reference burden in P4: 13 occurrences of '#2-9' (anchor refs) and 14 occurrences of 'Section 2.9' (prose refs) exist across sections.json. P4's 'operations' list backward-compat-anchor preserves URL bookmarks but does NOT sweep prose references that would now point to the new 2.9 ('Gravity Gradient with Latitude') instead of V13. Material refactor burden under-specified. See HOLE-04.

P0 operations reference '<!-- ═══ Part 2b ═══ -->' as a Part 2 boundary marker, but the comment currently sits INSIDE the cosmological-cluster's <div class="ps-detail"> at offset 58,682 — not at a true Part 2 boundary. Integrator should be aware that 'paste immediately before the closing HTML-comment' is ambiguous. See HOLE-01.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: EXP-450 is a prose-only proposal; no monitor.py interaction.

holes_found.description:
P0 operation 4 instructs 'paste the Summary AFTER the 2.12 cosmological-cluster block, immediately before the closing HTML-comment .' However, the current location of that HTML comment (sections.json:part2.html offset 58,682) is INSIDE the cosmological-cluster's
block, not at a true Part 2 boundary — the structure ends '...less selective.

\n\n
'. The comment functions as a navigational marker for the next part but is structurally nested inside the previous subsection. Following the instruction literally would paste the Summary INSIDE the cosmological-cluster block. The integrator should either (a) first relocate the comment to true end-of-part-2 (outside the cosmological-cluster ), then insert the Summary before it, or (b) simply append the Summary at end-of-string in part2.html. Operationally fixable in <5 minutes.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten P0's operation 4 to 'append the Summary block at end-of-string in part2.html (after the cosmological-cluster's closing </details>); relocate the <!-- ═══ Part 2b ═══ --> marker to follow the Summary block.' Add this as a fifth bullet under P0.operations.

holes_found.description: The 2.13 κ-divide-by-zero subsection uses the rhetorical headline 'treating it as 1.67 nT/µGal is treating 1/0 as a finite number.' This is a metaphorical compression of the actual argument (the dome's later high-precision data invalidates the κ derivation's denominator at the precision the dome itself cites). Literally, κ was computed as −10.9 nT ÷ −6.5 µGal from Mohe 1997 — not by division by zero. A defender will jiu-jitsu: 'You misrepresent our derivation; we never divided by zero.' The underlying argument is strong (location-dependent κ destroys PRED-TIER-3 going forward, fixed-κ contradicts the dome's own later 0-µGal confirmations) but the rhetorical headline opens an unnecessary strawman attack-surface. Also: WIN-013/014 verdicts in our review are 'Refuted by Data,' not 'Self-Contradicted' — the EXP correctly says the dome lists them as confirmations (true of dome's claims) but a careful defender may try to muddle 'dome-claimed' with 'our-verdict.'
holes_found.recommendation: Soften the literal divide-by-zero metaphor. Suggested rewording for paragraph 2: '...A coupling constant whose denominator is contradicted by the dome's own later high-precision confirmations cannot be treated as a fixed structural coefficient at the precision the dome itself cites.' Replace 'treating 1/0 as a finite number' with 'treating an empirically-contradicted denominator as a fixed coefficient.' Optionally add to paragraph 2: '(WIN-013/014 are listed by the dome as confirmations; our review verdicts both Refuted by Data, on the same data points.)' Substantive argument unchanged; strawman surface closed.

holes_found.description: The 2.14 ring-magnet subsection's third paragraph contains an EM-incorrect sub-claim: 'A ring or toroidal field with continuous flux conservation produces 1/r-type decay (or 1/r² in spherically symmetric cases), not exponential.' This is wrong. A magnetic dipole's far-field decays as 1/r³ (not 1/r); 1/r is the field of an infinite straight current (Ampère's law); 1/r² is the (non-existent) magnetic monopole or the radiation-zone field. A closed-loop ring magnet's external field is well-approximated as a dipole, with 1/r³ falloff far from the loop axis. A toroidal field outside the toroid is approximately zero (closed-loop flux containment). Any physicist-defender will spot this and use it to discredit the broader 1,660:1 argument — even though the flux-conservation core (Σ B·A = 0 around closed surface, area-weighted field equalization for poles, B_south/B_pole = A_pole/A_south) is mathematically correct. The decay-form sub-claim is unnecessary scaffolding around a correct headline argument.
holes_found.recommendation: Delete the third paragraph entirely ('The exponential form is also incompatible...'). The flux-conservation argument in paragraphs 1-2 stands on its own and is the strongest version of WIN-053. If a form-of-decay argument is desired, it should reference WIN-053's actual derivation and use 'inverse-cubic dipole far-field' (1/r³) — but the simpler path is to omit the decay-form discussion and rest on the magnitude self-contradiction. The 1,660:1 ratio between dome-topology and dome-fit B_south is the load-bearing claim; the exponential-vs-physical-form claim is dispensable and currently wrong.

holes_found.description: P4 (promote V13 from 2.9 to 2.1) underspecifies the cross-reference sweep burden. Quantitative scan of current sections.json shows: 13 occurrences of '#2-9' (anchor references) and 14 occurrences of 'Section 2.9' (prose references) across all parts. Of these, Part 1 alone contains 3 anchor refs (#2-9, #2-4) and 4 'Section 2.X' prose refs. P4's operations include backward-compat alias insertion, which preserves URL bookmarks — but prose references like 'see Section 2.9 for the V13 derivation' elsewhere in the review would now point to 'Gravity Gradient with Latitude' (the NEW 2.9, formerly 2.8) instead of V13. The integrator's work scope is therefore: (a) the renumber/relocate of P4.operations as described, PLUS (b) a sweep of ~27 cross-reference sites across sections.json updating subsection-number references in prose. This work is unaccounted for in the EXP. Combined with the operator-choice-required flagging, the lighter alternative (italic-strength-emphasis on existing 2.9, no renumbering) is materially more attractive than the EXP's framing suggests.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) add Step 5 to P4.operations: 'Sweep cross-references across all sections.json parts — update every "Section 2.X" prose reference and "#2-X" anchor reference in patches as required by the renumbering. Inventory before edit: scan with `grep -oE "Section 2\\.[1-9]\\b|#2-[1-9]\\b" data/sections.json | sort | uniq -c` to enumerate sites.' Or (b) update the operator-choice-required framing to strongly recommend the lighter alternative (option ii) given the cross-reference burden, retaining the full P4 promotion only if operator explicitly accepts the ~27-site refactor. Curmudgeon recommendation: prefer (b) — the lighter alternative achieves the within-tier-ranking signal at a fraction of the refactor cost and preserves backward compatibility of in-prose references.

holes_found.description: P5 (Tier-1 expansion to include 1.5/1.7/1.8/1.9) silently narrows the parent c2 review's identification, which named 1.5/1.6/1.7/1.8/1.9 (five sections including 1.6 Dielectric Foundation) as containing Tier-1-class attacks. EXP P5 includes 1.5/1.7/1.8/1.9 (four sections, dropping 1.6) without explaining the asymmetry. Adversarial review of section 1.6 confirms the narrowing IS defensible — 1.6 reads as a critique of the dome's interpretation of real observations ('the observations are real; the dome interpretation adds nothing the standard model doesn't already explain'), which is Tier-2-style reasoning (compare-with-standard-physics), NOT a Tier-1-style structural-attack-using-dome-parameters. However, the EXP does NOT document this narrowing. A future reader/reviewer reading the c2 review alongside the EXP will be confused why 1.6 is missing. A one-sentence justification in the EXP or in P5's `addresses_holes` rationale would close the gap.
holes_found.recommendation: Add to P5 either (a) an `exclusion_rationale` field: 'Section 1.6 (Dielectric Foundation) is excluded from the Tier-1 expansion. It critiques the dome's interpretation of real observations (Tier-2-style) rather than attacking the dome's framework using dome-published parameters (Tier-1-style). The c2 reviewer's listing of 1.6 in the 1.5–1.9 set is corrected here.' Or (b) a one-line note in the EXP's `anticipated_objections` array addressing 'Why is 1.6 missing from the Tier-1 list?' The patch itself is correct as written; only documentation is needed.

holes_found.description: P6's proposed insertion contains a count-scope mismatch: 'The case below is overdetermined: any one of the {{SELF_CONTRADICTED}} self-contradictions in Part 2 alone falsifies the model...' The templater token {{SELF_CONTRADICTED}} resolves to 20 (the data/wins.json verdict count, ALL self-contradictions identified across the entire review). However, Part 2 currently surfaces only 12 of these as named subsection-level derivations (the 9 numbered 2.1–2.9 plus 3 post-Summary loose args). After P1–P3 land, Part 2 surfaces 15. The other 5–8 self-contradictions live in WIN cards alone (Part 3) and are not 'in Part 2.' A careful defender will say: 'The prose claims 20 SCs in Part 2 but only ~12 are actually there. The argument is inflated by 60–80%.' This is a real attack-surface that undermines the very 'overdetermined' framing the patch tries to establish. The fix is small: change 'in Part 2' to 'identified in this review.'
holes_found.recommendation:
Change P6.replacement_html_insertion to: '

A note on argument abundance. The case below is overdetermined: any one of the {{SELF_CONTRADICTED}} self-contradictions identified in this review alone falsifies the model, and the External Falsification Tests in Part 7 provide independent confirmation from data the dome did not choose. The argument hierarchy that follows is organized for reader navigation, not because the conclusion needs every argument. A reader who reads only the Tier-1 self-contradictions in Parts 2 and 2b finds the model already refutes itself; the lower tiers exist for completeness and to engage the dome\'s own framing of "kill-shot binary tests."

' (Single edit: 'in Part 2 alone' → 'identified in this review alone'.) Also delete the templater-token-mapping caveat (P6.template_token_note) — verified that {{SELF_CONTRADICTED}} is already wired globally in build-scripts/generate-html.js:82; no per-section registration needed.

holes_found.description: EXP's `recommended_decider_action.primary` says 'Push P0+P1+P2+P3 (Part 2 internal restructure) to the curmudgeon priority queue as a single coordinated bundle.' But the sequencing_note in P4 says 'If P4 executes, it should land BEFORE P0-P3 because P0-P3 anchor on "after 2-12-cosmological-cluster" which is stable under renumbering, but the V13 subsection's identity itself moves under P4.' These two statements together leave the decider with ambiguity: does the P0-P3 bundle land first OR does P4 land first? If operator approves P4, the EXP suggests P4 first; if operator selects lighter alternative or defers, P0-P3 land unchanged. The EXP should make this contingency explicit so the decider's queue-population action doesn't need to wait on operator P4 decision.
holes_found.recommendation: Add to `recommended_decider_action`: 'Sequencing contingency: (1) Push P0+P1+P2+P3+P5+P6 to the queue as the default bundle (class=holistic). (2) Open an operator-attention note for P4 with the cross-reference burden estimate (~27 sites) and the lighter alternative (italic-emphasis on existing 2.9). (3) If operator approves P4 full promotion, land P4 FIRST (separate queue push, class=deep-attack due to refactor scope), then re-confirm P0–P3 anchor stability before the bundle lands. (4) If operator selects lighter alternative or defers, the P0–P3 bundle lands unchanged.'

stronger_arguments.description: The 1,660:1 ring-magnet argument (P2) becomes substantially stronger if the EXP drops the decay-form sub-claim and adds a single explicit equation. Suggested: 'The flux-conservation constraint is Σ_loop B·dA = constant around any closed magnetic surface (Gauss\'s law for magnetism, ∇·B = 0). For the dome\'s claimed ring topology: B_south × A_south = B_pole × A_pole. With A_pole ≈ π(10³ km)² ≈ 3.1 × 10⁶ km² and A_south ≈ 2π × 20,015 km × H_firmament (~ 5 × 10⁹ km² at H ≈ 40,000 km), the ratio A_pole/A_south ≈ 6 × 10⁻⁴, forcing B_south/B_pole ≤ 6 × 10⁻⁴. The dome\'s fitted B_pole ≈ 65 µT gives B_south ≤ 39 nT; the fitted B(r) equation gives 64,852 nT. The dome\'s topology and its curve-fit disagree by 3 orders of magnitude.' This sharper version eliminates the EM-imprecise '1/r-type decay' framing and provides the actual derivation a reader can verify.

The κ-divide-by-zero argument (P1) becomes more durable if framed as 'denominator non-replication + later precision-improved nulls' rather than 'literal divide-by-zero.' Suggested headline rephrase: '2.13 The κ Coupling Constant: Denominator Contradicted by the Dome\'s Own Later Data.' Substantively the same; rhetorically less attackable.

kernel_of_truth.description: The EXP correctly identifies a real sub-hierarchy bug in Part 2. The c2 review's macrostructure-right-sub-hierarchy-wrong framing is accurate: the V6 restructure (2026-04-07) correctly elevated self-contradictions to Part 2 (HOLISTIC-HOL-HIERARCHY.c2.json hole 1 confirms macrostructure improvement), but Part 2 internal ordering remains awkward — V13 (the foundation argument for every dome distance, per WIN-029 and the dome's own monitor.py coordinate computation) sits at 2.9 not 2.1 (current sections.json:part2.html offset 23,861 vs 2.1 Schumann at offset before 23,861), the Summary block sits mid-section at offset 39,405 with three substantive arguments after it (axial-symmetry offset 41,146, sign-only 45,668, cosmological-cluster 52,788), and 8 of the 20 verdict-tagged SCs (data/wins.json verdict count = 20) are absent from the Part 2 structural arc (currently 12 named/loose vs 20 in data — gap of 8). All six patches target real defects identified in HOLISTIC-HOL-HIERARCHY.c2.json. The proposal's anticipated_objections array (5 entries) addresses the most likely defender rebuttals with substantive responses — particularly objection #1 (goalpost-moving) is correctly answered with 'the SCs being surfaced are not new attacks; they are pre-existing wins.json:verdict=Self-Contradicted entries that have always existed in data, just absent from the Part 2 narrative.'
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Macrostructure-right-execution-needs-polish is a fair characterization of the EXP's contribution as well: the proposal correctly identifies real defects (HOLISTIC-HOL-HIERARCHY.c2.json hole 0–6) and proposes the right kind of fix for each, but three of the six patches contain substantive issues (EXP450-HOLE-03 P2 EM-imprecise decay form; EXP450-HOLE-04 P4 cross-reference burden under-specified; EXP450-HOLE-06 P6 count-scope mismatch) that should be revised before the patches land. Two further patches need polishing (EXP450-HOLE-02 P1 rhetorical headline; EXP450-HOLE-05 P5 undocumented 1.6 exclusion). The kernel-of-truth in the EXP itself stands: HIER-PHASE2-01..04 (the 4 major holes) are real and the patch set is correctly targeted; what fails to save it without revision is execution quality on three of the six patches.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A defender reviewing the patch set would attack the WEAKEST claim first to discredit the WHOLE proposal — and the EM-incorrect 1/r-type-decay sub-claim in P2 is exactly such a weak link. A physicist defender would read paragraph 3 of the proposed 2.14, identify that 1/r decay is not the standard EM prediction for closed-loop ring topology (correct: 1/r³ dipole far-field), and use this single error to argue 'the reviewers don\'t understand basic electromagnetism; we should reject their entire 1,660:1 claim.' The defense survives ONE round if the EXP lands P2 as written. Mitigation: either drop the third paragraph entirely (recommendation in EXP450-HOLE-03) or rewrite it with the correct 1/r³ form. Defense does not survive AFTER mitigation because the flux-conservation core (paragraphs 1–2) is mathematically airtight. Similarly: a defender will exploit P6's '20 SCs in Part 2' count mismatch ('they claim 20 but only 12 are visible') unless the wording is fixed.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Before integration: (1) revise P2 to drop the decay-form paragraph (or correct it to 1/r³ dipole far-field); (2) revise P6 to change 'in Part 2' to 'identified in this review'; (3) revise P1's headline metaphor to remove the 'literal divide-by-zero' framing. After these three substantive edits the proposal package is integration-ready. The other holes (P0 comment placement, P4 cross-ref burden, P5 1.6 exclusion rationale, EXP-450-HOLE-07 sequencing contingency) are documentation-quality fixes that the decider can resolve in queue ordering without further analyst revision.

checked: P1 referenced WINs (012/013/014): WIN-012 verdict=Self-Contradicted (matches EXP), WIN-013/014 verdict=Refuted by Data (EXP correctly distinguishes 'dome-claimed confirmations' from 'our verdicts' — clarification recommended in EXP450-HOLE-02).
P2 referenced WIN (053): verdict=Self-Contradicted, finding contains 1,660 and 64,852 and 39 nT and flux (matches EXP).
P3 referenced WIN (044): verdict=Self-Contradicted, finding contains 28% and 5,715 and 9,983 km (matches EXP).
PRED-TIER-3 cross-reference: exists with August 2026 eclipse + 10–13 µGal gravity prediction (matches EXP).
Part 6.14 κ Coupling Cluster cross-reference: exists at offset 117,219 (matches EXP).
inconsistencies_found: EXP400-HOLE-05 (logged above): EXP P5 silently narrows the c2 review's 5-section Tier-1 list to 4 sections (drops 1.6 Dielectric Foundation). Defensible narrowing but undocumented.

deeper_analysis:
EXP-450 is a well-structured holistic proposal package — analyst-mode-1-holistic correctly assembled 6 patches against 7 holes identified in the c2 review, with explicit hole-to-patch mapping, anticipated objections, quantitative verification, and recommended-decider-action. The kernel-of-truth (V6 macrostructure right, sub-hierarchy needs polish) is fair. The proposal's verdict-count consistency check (SC=20 unchanged, Part 2 surfaced count moving 12→15) and the templater token discipline ({{SELF_CONTRADICTED}} usage) show good cross-section consistency awareness.

However, three patches contain substantive issues that a defender would exploit:

(1) **P2's 1/r-decay claim is EM-incorrect.** This is the most serious hole — a physicist-defender will identify the EM error in seconds and use it to discredit the 1,660:1 argument even though the underlying flux-conservation argument is mathematically correct. The fix is small (drop one paragraph) but essential. Magnetic dipoles fall off as 1/r³, not 1/r; the latter is the field of an infinite straight current (Ampère's law, not closed-loop topology). The cleanest version of WIN-053's argument rests purely on Σ B·A = 0 (flux conservation) and the area-ratio between the Axis Mundi aperture and the Antarctic-barrier circumferential band. The decay-form sub-claim is unnecessary scaffolding.

(2) **P4's cross-reference burden is under-specified.** Promoting V13 from 2.9 to 2.1 renumbers 9 subsections and requires updating ~27 cross-reference sites elsewhere in sections.json (13 anchor refs '#2-9' + 14 prose refs 'Section 2.9'). The EXP's `operations` only lists in-place renumbering + backward-compat alias anchors. The alias anchors preserve URL bookmarks but NOT prose references — a reader following 'see Section 2.9 for the V13 derivation' elsewhere in the review would now land on 'Gravity Gradient with Latitude' (the new 2.9, formerly 2.8). This work needs to be either added to P4's operations OR the operator-choice framing needs to strongly recommend the lighter alternative.

(3) **P6's count-scope mismatch.** {{SELF_CONTRADICTED}}=20 (data/wins.json verdict count, all SCs across the review) but Part 2 surfaces only 12 (current) or 15 (after P1-P3 land). The prose 'any one of the {{SELF_CONTRADICTED}} self-contradictions in Part 2 alone falsifies the model' creates a count mismatch a defender will exploit. One-word fix: 'in Part 2' → 'identified in this review.'

The other patches (P0 renumber-and-relocate-Summary, P1 κ-2.13, P3 FSF-2.15, P5 Tier-1-expansion) are substantively correct with minor polishing recommendations. P0 has a small ambiguity in the HTML-comment placement instruction (operationally fixable in <5 minutes). P1's 'literal divide-by-zero' headline metaphor is rhetorically strong but invites a strawman attack; softening to 'denominator contradicted by dome's own later data' preserves substance and closes the attack-surface. P3 is solid as written. P5 silently drops Section 1.6 from the parent c2 review's 5-section list, which IS defensible (1.6 is Tier-2-style 'real observations + bad interpretation,' not Tier-1-style 'structural attack using dome parameters') but needs documentation so future reviewers reading c2 and EXP-450 side-by-side don't get confused.

**Sequencing concern (EXP450-HOLE-07):** The EXP's `recommended_decider_action.primary` proposes a single P0+P1+P2+P3 bundle, but P4's `sequencing_note` implies P4 should land first IF executed. These two statements aren't reconciled; the decider needs explicit contingency on operator P4 decision. Recommend: push P0+P1+P2+P3+P5+P6 as a single bundle (class=holistic), defer P4 to operator-attention with cross-ref burden estimate, and only land P4 if operator explicitly approves the full refactor.

**Verdict for the decider:** recommended_action=minor_edit. Three substantive edits required (P2 paragraph 3 drop, P4 cross-ref sweep specification OR lighter-alternative recommendation, P6 'in Part 2' → 'identified in this review'). Three documentation-quality edits recommended (P0 comment placement clarification, P1 headline softening, P5 1.6-exclusion rationale). After these revisions the proposal is integration-ready. The kernel-of-truth (real sub-hierarchy bug, correctly-targeted patches) survives intact; only execution-quality on three patches needs revision.

summary_for_decider: EXP-450 is a structurally sound holistic proposal (6 patches addressing 7 c2-identified hierarchy holes) with quantitative verification CONFIRMED on all checked claims. The proposal's macrostructure-right-sub-hierarchy-needs-polish framing is fair. RECOMMENDED ACTION: minor_edit before integration. Three substantive revisions required: (P2) drop the EM-incorrect '1/r-type decay' paragraph from the 2.14 ring-magnet subsection — rely purely on flux conservation, which is mathematically airtight; (P4) either specify the ~27-site cross-reference sweep in operations OR strongly recommend the lighter alternative (italic-emphasis on existing 2.9, no renumbering); (P6) change 'any one of the {{SELF_CONTRADICTED}} self-contradictions in Part 2 alone' to 'identified in this review alone' to close a 20-vs-12-or-15 count mismatch. Three documentation revisions recommended: (P0) tighten HTML-comment placement instruction to 'append at end-of-string + relocate the Part 2b boundary marker'; (P1) soften the literal 'divide-by-zero' headline metaphor to 'denominator-contradicted-by-later-data'; (P5) add a one-sentence rationale for excluding Section 1.6 from the Tier-1 expansion. SEQUENCING: push P0+P1+P2+P3+P5+P6 as a single class=holistic bundle (default path); defer P4 to operator-attention with cross-ref burden estimate (lighter alternative materially more attractive than EXP framing suggests); the queue-pop on this review should also close ISS-2273..2279 contingent on the revised patches landing. The advocate-mode pass identifies the P2 1/r-decay claim and the P6 count mismatch as the two defender-attack-surfaces; both are easily closed with the recommended edits. Cycle 1 of this proposal review; recommend the analyst (or decider via self-apply) write a c2 revision applying the 6 hole recommendations.
